title: Anchor devces and methods of use
abstract: The method of fixing soft tissue to bone includes placing an anchor device superficial to a segment of soft tissue superficially juxtaposed to a unicortical blind hole in a bone, the anchor device having a relaxed state characterized by a first outer dimension. The method includes driving the soft tissue segment into the blind hole using the anchor device. The anchor device transiently constricts to a constricted state during insertion through the blind hole, the constricted state characterized by a second outer dimension that is smaller than the first outer dimension. The method includes trapping a portion of the soft tissue segment between a portion of the anchor device and a portion of the bone when at least a portion of the anchor device is disposed sub-cortically and relaxes towards the relaxed state. Related devices, systems and methods are also described.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of fixing soft tissue to bone, comprising: placing an anchor device superficial to a segment of soft tissue superficially juxtaposed to a unicortical blind hole in a bone, the anchor device having a relaxed state characterized by a first outer dimension; driving the soft tissue segment into the blind hole using the anchor device, wherein the anchor device transiently constricts to a constricted state during insertion through the blind hole, the constricted state characterized by a second outer dimension that is smaller than the first outer dimension; and trapping a portion of the soft tissue segment between a portion of the anchor device and a portion of the bone when at least a portion of the anchor device is disposed sub- cortically and relaxes towards the relaxed state. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the anchor device is fabricated from one or more biocompatible polymers. 3. The method of claims 1 or 2, wherein the biocompatible polymers include Polyetherether ketone (PEEK) and ultrahigh molecular weight polypropylene (UHMWPE). 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein the anchor device is fabricated from one or more absorbable biocompatible polymers. 5. The method of claims 1 or 4, wherein the absorbable biocompatible polymers include polyglycolic acid (PGA), polylactic acid (PLA), polydioxanone, caprolactone, or a combination thereof. 6. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the soft tissue is a tendon or a ligament. 7. An anchor device for attaching a soft tissue segment within bone, the anchor device comprising: a body having a distal end region and a proximal end region each at least partially surrounding an interior volume forming a slot extending generally along a longitudinal axis 

 of the body; and a concave saddle surface at the distal end region of the body configured to receive and redirect a soft tissue segment extending through the slot in a first direction from the proximal end region of the body to a second direction that is at an angle to the first direction. 8. The anchor device of claim 7, wherein the distal end region of the body is configured to be positioned sub-cortically to a bone defect and at least a portion of the proximal end region of the body is configured to be positioned intra-cortically within the bone defect. 9. The anchor device of claims 7 or 8, wherein during insertion of the anchor device into the bone defect the body is configured to transiently constrict from a relaxed state having a first outer dimension to a constricted state having a second outer dimension that is smaller than the first outer dimension. 10. The anchor device of any one of claims 7-9, wherein the soft tissue segment is a tendon or a ligament. 11. The anchor device of any one of claims 7-10, wherein at least a portion of the soft tissue segment is trapped between a portion of the anchor device and a portion of the bone defect when at least a portion of the anchor device is disposed sub-cortically and relaxes towards the relaxed state. 12. The method of any one of claims 7-11, wherein the anchor device is fabricated from one or more biocompatible polymers. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the biocompatible polymers include Polyetherether ketone (PEEK) and ultrahigh molecular weight polypropylene (UHMWPE). 14. The method of any one of claims 7-11, wherein the anchor device is fabricated from one or more absorbable biocompatible polymers. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the absorbable biocompatible polymers include polyglycolic acid (PGA), polylactic acid (PLA), polydioxanone, caprolactone, or a combination thereof. 16. An anchor device for attaching a soft tissue segment within bone having a cortex and a sub-cortical region, the anchor device comprising: 

 a body having a distal end region and a proximal end region each at least partially surrounding an interior volume forming a slot extending generally along a longitudinal axis of the body, wherein the body is configured to be transiently constricted by a cortical aperture within the bone from a relaxed state having a first outer dimension to a constricted state having a second outer dimension that is smaller than the first outer dimension such that the soft tissue segment is trapped between at least a portion of the body and at least a portion of the bone cortex upon subcortical relaxation of the body towards the relaxed state. 17. The anchor device of claim 16, further comprising a concave saddle surface at the distal end region of the body configured to receive and redirect the soft tissue segment extending through the slot in a first direction from the proximal end region of the body to a second direction that is at an angle to the first direction. 18. The anchor device of claims 16 or 17, further comprising a pair of opposed prongs extending from the distal end region of the body on either side of the midline axis and defining the concave saddle surface. 19. The anchor device of any one of claims 16-18, wherein the slot provides a generally c-shaped cross-section to at least a portion of the body. 20. The anchor device of claim 19, further comprising a proximally-extending cortical feature near the proximal end region of the body, the cortical feature having an arcuate length and cross-sectional size that is smaller than an arcuate length and cross- sectional size of the proximal end region of the body forming a cortical rim retention shelf near the proximal end region of the body. 21. The anchor device of claim 20, wherein the body has a tapered sidewall between the cortical rim retention shelf to the distal end region of the body. 22. The anchor device of claims 20 or 21, wherein the cortical rim retention shelf contracts radially around the longitudinal axis when in the constricted state. 23. The anchor device of any of claims 20-22, wherein at least a portion of the anchor device flexes in an arcuate manner when in the constricted state. 24. The anchor device of any of claims 20-23, wherein the cortical rim retention 

 shelf further comprises a proximal aspect forming an edge having a plurality of spikes, serrations, or projecting elements, and wherein the proximal aspect captures soft tissue between the proximal end region of the device and at least a portion of bone through which the device is implanted. 25. The anchor device of claim 16, wherein the body is monolithic and has a substantially conical or frusto-conical shape. 26. The anchor device of claim 25, wherein the body is generally pliable and elastic and has a transverse cross-sectional geometry that is generally arcuate-shaped forming a concave surface and a convex surface to the body. 27. The anchor device of claim 26, wherein an arcuate length of the transverse cross-section near the distal end region of the body is shorter than an arcuate length of the transverse cross-section near the proximal end region of the body. 28. The anchor device of claim 27, wherein when in use within a bored defect within the bone, the distal end region of the body is positioned within a depth of the medullary cavity of the bone and the proximal end region of the body is positioned within the bone near a cortical surface of the bored defect. 29. The anchor device of claim 28, wherein the soft tissue segment is a tendon or a ligament having an attached end and a detached end, wherein the soft tissue segment is positioned along the longitudinal axis of the body coursing along both the concave and the convex surfaces of the body such that soft tissue segment is sharply redirected by wrapping around the distal end region of the body and the detached end is compressed between the convex surface of the body near the proximal end region and the cortical surface of the bored defect. 30. The anchor device of claim 29, wherein the arcuate length of the transverse cross-section near the proximal end region of the body is greater than a diameter of the bored defect. 31. An anchor device for attaching materials within bone, the anchor device comprising: a body having a distal end region, a proximal end region, and a plurality of struts 

 extending between the distal end region to the proximal end region and at least partially surrounding an interior volume of the body; and an attachment feature positioned within the interior volume of the body and coupled near the distal end region, the attachment feature configured to secure material to the body; and wherein upon removal of a constraint and after delivery of the anchor device into bone the body passively transitions from a constrained, delivery configuration that is radially contracted and axially elongated to a relaxed, deployment configuration that is radially expanded and axially shortened. 32. The anchor device of claim 31, wherein the material secured by the attachment feature to the anchor device is suture or cable material. 33. The anchor device of claims 31 or 32, wherein the material is further affixed to a soft tissue structure that is selected from the group consisting of tendon, ligament, and joint capsule. 34. The anchor device of any one of claims 31-33, wherein the attachment feature comprises an element selected from the group consisting of a suture anchor element, a cleat element, a post, a saddle-shaped element, a pulley, and a crimping element. 35. The anchor device of claim 31, wherein the attachment feature comprises a post extending transverse to the longitudinal axis of the body. 36. The anchor device of claim 31, wherein the attachment feature comprises a saddle shaped element to which the material is secured. 37. The anchor device of claim 31, wherein the attachment feature comprises a cleat element to secure the material. 38. The anchor device of claim 37, wherein the cleat element employs a cam action or ratcheting reel assembly to progressively tension the material and approximate the material to the proximal end region of the body. 39. The anchor device of claim 37, wherein the cleat element comprises at least one suture anchor element, two apertures and an intervening central post. 40. The anchor device of claim 39, wherein a first portion of the material overlaps 

 a second portion of the material resulting in a unidirectional tensioning mechanism of the material with the cleat element. 41. The anchor device of claim 37, wherein the cleat element comprises at least two suture anchor elements, each having an aperture configured to allow the material to extend through. 42. The anchor device of claim 41 , wherein applying tension to the material forces the at least two suture anchor elements to form a splayed configuration. 43. The anchor device of claim 42, wherein the tension applied to the material is maintained by the at least two suture anchor elements wherein at least a part of the material passed through the apertures of the at least two suture anchor elements and wrapped around the commonly formed post results in a portion of the material overlapping another portion of the material. 44. The anchor device of claim 31, wherein the attachment feature comprises a crimping element to secure the material, wherein the material is attached to a soft tissue structure. 45. The anchor device of claim 44, wherein the material is secured with an interference pin delivered through an opening in the proximal end region of the body. 46. The anchor device of claim 45, wherein a proximal aspect of the crimping element includes a cable or suture transecting feature. 47. The anchor device of any one of claims 31-46, further comprising a penetrating tip coupled to the distal end region of the body. 48. The anchor device of claim 47, wherein the penetrating tip has a trephine, fluted or conically-tapered outer geometry to facilitate penetration of bone. 49. The anchor device of claims 47 or 48, wherein the material comprises a tensioning element configured to approximate the distal end region and the proximal end region upon application of tension on the material causing the plurality of struts to radially expand. 50. The anchor device of any of claims 47-49, wherein the penetrating tip and the tensioning element are integrated with the attachment feature forming an inner body 

 extending within the internal volume and surrounded at least in part by the body. 51. The anchor device of any one of claims 31-50, wherein the proximal end region comprises a discontinuous outer wall defining a proximal opening to the interior volume of the body within which the material is disposed such that soft tissue affixed to the material is in direct contact with the bone. 52. The anchor device of any one of claims 31-51, wherein the plurality of struts expand near the proximal end region to a greater degree than the plurality of struts expand near the distal end region. 53. The anchor device of any one of claims 31-52, wherein the body is fabricated from a superelastic metal. 54. The anchor device of any one of claims 31-53, wherein the constraint comprises a generally rigid tubular element. 55. The anchor device of claim 54, wherein the generally rigid tubular element comprises a slot to accommodate the material. 56. The anchor device of claim 54, wherein the constraint comprises a circumferential ring element. 57. The anchor device of claim 56, wherein the circumferential ring element is positioned between the material and the bone thereby preventing abrasion of the material, wherein the material comprises suture, cable or soft tissue. 58. The anchor device of any one of claims 31-57, wherein the constraint includes a primary constraint and further comprises a secondary constraint. 59. The anchor device of claim 58, wherein the secondary constraint is positioned over at least a region of the primary constraint during storage of the anchor device. 60. A suture or soft tissue anchor device comprising a self-expanding device configured to be constrained prior to delivery having a relatively reduced diameter with a relatively extended length, and subsequently expanded to a relatively larger diameter with a relatively shortened length after deployment and delivery within bone. 61. The device of claim 60, wherein the self-expanding device comprises a generally cylindrical or truncated cylindrical shaped body having two or more slots 

 configured to be constrained prior to delivery. 62. A suture or soft tissue anchor device configured to employ a self-expanding device that is constrained prior to delivery, in a relatively reduced diameter and relatively extended length, with subsequent deployment and delivery within bone, where it is configured to expand to a relatively larger diameter with a relatively shortened length; in which suture material or cable attached to the soft tissue being approximated or secured to the bone anchor is affixed or otherwise associated with the distal terminus or an element associated with the distal terminus of the anchor. 63. A suture or soft tissue anchor device configured to employ a self-expanding device that is constrained prior to delivery, in a relatively reduced diameter and relatively extended length, with subsequent deployment and delivery within bone, where it expands to a relatively larger diameter with a relatively shortened length; in which suture material or cable attached to the soft tissue being approximated or secured to the bone anchor is affixed or otherwise associated with the distal terminus or an element associated with the distal terminus of the anchor, such that tension applied on the suture or cable results in a force that foreshortens the length and expands the diameter of the anchor. 64. The device of claim 63, wherein the device is fabricated from a superelastic metal. 65. The device of claim 64, wherein the superelastic metal is nitinol. 66. The device of any one of claims 63-65, wherein a suture affixed to a soft tissue structure is secured to the distal end of the self-expanding implant. 67. The device of claim 66, wherein the soft tissue structure is a tendon, ligament, or joint capsule. 68. The device of any one of claims 63-67, wherein the self-expanding device is further expanded by means of a tensioning element that approximates the distal and proximal ends of the device. 69. The device of any one of claims 63-68, further comprising a distal tip that is conically tapered to facilitate penetration of bone. 70. The device of claim 69, wherein the distal tip is configured with trephine or 

 fluted geometry to facilitate penetration of bone. 71. The device of any one of claims 63-70, wherein suture affixed to a soft tissue structure are passed through an aperture located distally within the device and delivered through the proximal aperture of the device for subsequent tensioning. 72. The device of claim 71, wherein a cam action cleat mechanism is used to progressively tension the sutures and approximate the attached soft tissue element to the proximal aspect of the self-expanding device. 73. The device of claim 71, wherein a ratcheting reel assembly is used to tension the sutures and approximate the attached soft tissue structure to the proximal aspect of the self-expanding device. 74. The device of any one of claims 63-73, wherein the sutures coursing within the device and attached to a soft tissue structure are secured to the device with a crimping element. 75. The device of claim 74, wherein the proximal aspect of the crimping mechanism includes a cable or suture transecting feature. 76. The device of any one of claims 63-75, wherein the sutures coursing within the device and attached to a soft tissue structure are secured with an interference pin, delivered within the proximal aperture. 77. The device of claim 76, wherein the interference pin has a tapered distal tip. 78. The device of claim 76, wherein the cable or sutures restrained by the interference pin are transected immediately proximal or adjacent to the proximal aspect of the interference pin with a cable or suture cutter having a rotary actuation mechanism. 79. The device of any one of claims 63-78, wherein a generally tubular configured confinement element is used to maintain the self-expanding device in its confined geometry prior to distal delivery out of the confinement tube and into the bone. 80. The device of claim 79, wherein the primary confinement tube has a slot to accommodate introduction of suture material attached to a soft tissue structure. 81. The device of claim 79, wherein a secondary confinement tubing or ring element is positioned over the distal segment of the primary confinement tube during storage 

 and removed after chilling the self-expanding device immediately or shortly prior to deployment within the bone. 82. The device of claim 79, wherein a secondary confinement tubing is positioned over the distal segment of the primary confinement tube during storage and removed after chilling the self-expanding device immediately or shortly prior to deployment within the bone. 83. The device of any one of claims 63-82, wherein the device includes at least one suture anchor element having two apertures and an intervening central post, to allow suture to extend through the apertures and around the central post such that a portion of the suture overlaps another portion of the suture, resulting in a unidirectional tensioning mechanism of the suture or cleat mechanism. 84. The device of claim 83, wherein the device employs a cam action cleat mechanism to progressively tension the sutures and approximate the attached soft tissue element to the proximal aspect of the self-expanding device. 85. The device of claim 83, wherein the unidirectional tensioning mechanism comprises at least two suture anchor elements, each having an aperture and a commonly formed post comprising at least one post element from each of the at least two suture anchor elements, wherein the suture is passed through the apertures of the at least two suture anchor elements and wrapped around the common post. 86. The device of claim 83, wherein the device includes at least two suture anchor elements, each having an aperture configured to allow suture to extend through. 87. The device of claim 86, wherein applying tension to the suture forces the at least two suture anchor elements to form a splayed configuration. 88. The device of claim 87, wherein the tension applied to the suture is maintained by the at least two suture anchor elements wherein at least a part of the suture passed through the apertures of the at least two suture anchor elements and wrapped around the commonly formed post, results in a portion of the suture overlapping another portion of the same suture. 89. A self-expanding suture anchor device comprising a pre-deployment confined configuration which is at least in part maintained by a circumferential ring element, which in 

 the deployed state of the suture anchor device, serves to provide a suture abrasion protective function resulting from its surface features and deployment position, located between the suture material and the bone. 90. An anchor device for attaching tissue within bone, the anchor device comprising: a body having a distal end region, a proximal end region, and a plurality of struts extending between the distal end region to the proximal end region and at least partially surround an interior volume of the body, wherein the body passively transitions from a constrained, delivery configuration that is radially contracted and axially elongated to a relaxed, deployment configuration that is radially expanded and axially shortened upon removal of a constraint on the plurality of struts after delivery into bone; an attachment feature positioned within the interior volume of the body near the distal end region, the attachment feature configured to secure the tissue to the anchor device; and a distal penetrating tip, wherein the proximal end region comprises a discontinuous outer wall defining a proximal opening to the interior volume of the body within which the secured tissue is disposed so as to be in direct contact with the bone. 91. A method for anchoring soft tissue comprising: constraining a self-expanding anchor device within a lumen of a constraining element, the self-expanding anchor device comprising: a plurality of struts extending between a distal end region and a proximal end region of the anchor device and at least partially surrounding an internal volume of the anchor device; an attachment feature positioned near the distal end region of the anchor device; and a proximal opening into the internal volume, wherein at least a portion of the plurality of struts is constrained by the constraining element and at least a portion of the distal end region extends beyond a distal edge of the constraining element; 

 securing a material to the attachment feature and routing the material through the internal volume of the anchor device; penetrating a bone surface with the distal end region of the self-expanding anchor device until the distal edge of the constraining element abuts the bone surface; sliding an advancing element relative to the constraining element urging the anchor device beyond the distal end of the constraining element into a subcortical location of the bone surface; and passively expanding the plurality of struts within the subcortical location. 92. The method of claim 91, wherein the material comprises suture or cable. 93. The method of claim 91 or 92, wherein the material is secured to soft tissue. 94. An implantable fixation device formed at least in part of temperature affected shape set material that transitions from a geometrically confined configuration to an expanded configuration, wherein the device is constrained to the confined configuration at ambient storage temperatures by a removable element, and wherein the removable element is removed after the temperature affected shape set material is chilled immediately prior to delivery into the body. 95. The device of claim 94, wherein the removable element is generally tubular. 96. The device of claim 95, wherein a secondary confinement tubing or ring element is positioned over a distal segment of the removable element during storage and wherein the secondary confinement tubing is removed after chilling the self-expanding device immediately or shortly prior to deployment within the bone. 97. The device of any one of claims 94-96, wherein the implantable fixation device is an implantable soft tissue fixation device. 98. The device of any one of claims 31-59, wherein the proximal end region further comprises a plurality of serrations extending from a proximal edge of the device. 99. The device of any one of claims 60-61, wherein a proximal end region of the device further comprises a plurality of serrations extending from a proximal edge of the device. 100. The device of claim 99, wherein the plurality of serrations capture soft tissue between the proximal end region of the device and at least a portion of bone through which the device is implanted. 101. A suture or soft tissue anchor assembly, comprising: an anchor device comprising a plurality of self-expanding stays extending between a proximal end region and a distal terminus and surrounding an internal volume; and a soft tissue material extending through the internal volume and looped over the distal terminus of the anchor device, wherein the soft tissue material is approximated or secured to the anchor device at the distal terminus such that tension applied on the soft tissue material causes a force that foreshortens the length and expands the diameter of the anchor device. 102. The assembly of claim 101, wherein the proximal end region of the device further comprises a plurality of serrations extending proximally from a proximal edge of the device. 103. The assembly of claim 102, wherein the plurality of serrations capture the soft tissue material between the proximal end region of the device and at least a portion of bone through which the device is implanted. 104. The assembly of any one of claims 101-103, wherein the internal volume extends an entire length of the anchor device from the proximal end region through the distal end region. 105. The assembly of any one of claims 101-104, further comprising a cap covering an outer wall at the distal end region of the device. 106. The assembly of claim 105, wherein the cap comprises a surface edge that is broader than a bare edge of the outer wall, wherein the cap is configured to support and redirect the soft tissue material looped over the distal terminus. 107. The assembly of claims 105 or 106, wherein the cap is formed of a material that is softer than the material of the outer wall. 108. The assembly of any one of claims 105-107, wherein the cap is a polymeric material. 109. The assembly of any one of claims 105-108, wherein the cap further comprises one or more ridged surfaces. 110. The assembly of claim 109, wherein the one or more ridged surfaces are oriented in a direction that is transverse to a longitudinal axis of the internal volume through which the soft tissue material extends. 111. The assembly of claims 109 or 110, wherein the one or more ridged surfaces are configured to increase surface friction between the soft tissue material and the device to reduce soft tissue migration relative to the distal terminus. 112. An anchor device for attaching materials within bone, the anchor device comprising: a body having a distal end region, a proximal end region, and a tapered sidewall between the distal end region and the proximal end region, the body defining a bore extending along the body from the distal end region to the proximal end region; and a soft-tissue directing feature at the distal end region of the body configured to direct a soft-tissue that extends through the bore in a first direction to a second direction at an angle to the first direction, wherein the body of the anchor device is contracted from an initial configuration to a delivery configuration in which an external diameter of at least a portion of the body is reduced, and return to the initial configuration upon release. 113. The anchor device of claim 112, wherein the anchor device is contracted radially of the body. 114. The anchor device of claims 112 or 113, wherein the anchor device is contracted in an arcuate manner. 115. The anchor device of any one of claims 112-114, wherein at least the portion of the body has a c-shaped cross-section with an interrupted outer wall portion. 116. The anchor device of claim 115, wherein the interrupted outer wall portion has an interruption that is dimensioned to control a maximum reduction in the diameter of the body. 117. The anchor device of any one of claims 112-116, further comprising a soft- 

 tissue confinement feature at the distal end of the body and adjacent to the soft-tissue directing feature, the soft-tissue confinement feature being configured to confine the soft- tissue that passes through the bore of the body and directed by the soft-tissue directing feature. 118. The anchor device of claim 117, wherein the soft-tissue confinement feature comprises a pair of opposed prongs extending at the distal end of the body. 119. The anchor device of any one of claims 112-118, further comprising a soft- tissue compression collar at the proximal end portion of the body. 120. The anchor device of any one of claims 112-119, further comprising a retention shelf near the proximal end portion of the body, the tapered sidewall tapers from the retention shelf to the distal end portion. 121. The anchor device of claim 120, wherein the retention shelf comprises the portion of the body that is reduced when contracted. 122. The anchor device of any one of claims 112-121, further comprising a soft tissue-compression collar at the proximal end portion of the body, adjacent the retention shelf. 123. The anchor device of claim 122, wherein the retention shelf further comprises one or more ridging surfaces. 124. The anchor device of claim 123, wherein the one or more ridging are arcuate. 125. The anchor device of any one of claims 112-124, wherein the soft-tissue directing feature comprises one or more ridging to increase friction with the soft-tissue. 126. The anchor device of any one of claims 112-125, wherein the body is monolithic. 127. The anchor device of any one of claims 112-126, wherein the body has a substantially conical shape. 128. The anchor device of any one of claims 112-127, wherein the tapered sidewall comprises one or more scalloped features. 129. The anchor device of any one of claims 112-128, wherein the body of the 

 anchor device is further configured to flex generally along a longitudinal axis of the body. 130. A system for securing tendon to bone, comprising: a pliable and elastic body having a transverse cross-sectional geometry that is generally arcuate-shaped forming a concave and a convex surface to the body, the body having a generally orthogonally-oriented long axis extending between a distal end and a proximal end, wherein an arcuate length of the transverse cross-section near the distal end is shorter than an arcuate length of the transverse cross-section near the proximal end, wherein when in use within a bored defect within a bone, the distal end of the body is positioned within a depth of the medullary cavity of the bone and the proximal end of the body is positioned within the bone near a cortical surface of the bored defect; and a tendon positioned along the long axis of the body coursing along both the concave and the convex surfaces of the body such that the course of the tendon is sharply redirected by wrapping around the distal end of the body while a segment of a detached end of the tendon is compressed between the convex surface of the body near the proximal end and the cortical surface of the bored defect. 131. The system of claim 130, wherein the body has a generally hemi-frusto- conical geometry. 132. The system of claims 130 or 131, further comprising distally projecting elements forming a lateral confining element within which the tendon courses around the distal end of the body. 133. The system of any one of claims 130-132, wherein the arcuate length of the proximal end of the body is greater than a diameter of the bored defect. 134. The system of any one of claims 130-133, wherein a transverse arcuate wall thickness of the body is non-uniform. 135. The system of claim 134, wherein the transverse arcuate wall thickness is greater near a margin than in a central region. 136. The system of any one of claims 130-135, wherein the body is composed of elastomeric material. 137. The system of claim 136, wherein the elastomeric material is a 

 PolyEtherEtherKetone (PEEK) polymer or Nitinol alloy. 138. A pliable and elastic device for securing tendon to bone that is configured to be driven into a cortical cancellous bore initially resulting in radial contraction and arcuate hinging of the device through a region of the cortical bone with subsequent relaxation of the device in a region of subcortical bone. 139. The device of claim 138, wherein a diameter of a portion of the proximal subcortical arcuate segment of the device is greater than a diameter of the cortical bore through which the device is inserted. 140. The device of claims 138 or 139, wherein the device is composed of elastomeric material. 141. The device of claim 140, wherein the elastomeric material is a PEEK polymer or Nitinol alloy.
